From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Unionism (disambiguation)](/w/index.php?title=Unionism_(disambiguation)&redirect=no "Unionism (disambiguation)"))

[![logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/99/Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg/40px-Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Wiktionary-logo-en-v2.svg)

Look up ***[unionism](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/unionism "wiktionary:unionism")*** in Wiktionary, the free dictionary.

**Unionism** may refer to:

## Trades

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Unionism&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Trades")]

* [Community unionism](/wiki/Community_unionism "Community unionism"), the ways trade unions work with community organizations
* [Craft unionism](/wiki/Craft_unionism "Craft unionism"), a model of trade unionism in which workers are organised based on a particular craft or trade
* [Dual unionism](/wiki/Dual_unionism "Dual unionism"), the development of a union or political organization parallel to and within an existing labor union
* [Industrial unionism](/wiki/Industrial_unionism "Industrial unionism"), a labor union organizing method in which all workers in the same industry are organized into the same union
* [New Unionism](/wiki/New_Unionism "New Unionism"), a term which has been used twice in the history of the labour movement to describe moves to broaden the union agenda
* [Open-source unionism](/wiki/Open-source_unionism "Open-source unionism"), a term coined by academics Richard B. Freeman and Joel Rogers to explain a possible new model for organizing workers
* [Social Movement Unionism](/wiki/Social_Movement_Unionism "Social Movement Unionism"), a trend of theory and practice in contemporary trade unionism
* [Solidarity unionism](/wiki/Solidarity_unionism "Solidarity unionism"), a model of labour organizing in which the workers themselves formulate strategy and take action
* The organization of workers in [trade unions](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union")

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Unionism&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Politics")]

* Preference for maintaining a [political union](/wiki/Political_union "Political union") as opposed to [separatism](/wiki/Separatism "Separatism")
* [Unionism in Belgium](/wiki/Unionism_in_Belgium "Unionism in Belgium")
* [Union (American Civil War)](/wiki/Union_(American_Civil_War) "Union (American Civil War)")
* [Ittihadism](/wiki/Ittihadism "Ittihadism"), ideology of the Committee of Union and Progress in the Ottoman Empire
* [Unification of Moldova and Romania](/wiki/Unification_of_Moldova_and_Romania "Unification of Moldova and Romania"), advocates are referred to as "Unionists"
* [Albanian unionism](/wiki/Unification_of_Albania_and_Kosovo "Unification of Albania and Kosovo")
* [Serbian–Montenegrin unionism](/wiki/Serbian%E2%80%93Montenegrin_unionism "Serbian–Montenegrin unionism")
* [Spanish unionism](/wiki/Spanish_unionism "Spanish unionism")

### In the British isles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Unionism&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: In the British isles")]

* [Unionism in the United Kingdom](/wiki/Unionism_in_the_United_Kingdom "Unionism in the United Kingdom"), support for the UK constituent countries remaining part of the United Kingdom state
* [Unionism in Ireland](/wiki/Unionism_in_Ireland "Unionism in Ireland"), support for Ireland (mainly Northern Ireland) being part of the UK, rather than a Republic of Ireland or an independent Northern Ireland
* [Unionism in Scotland](/wiki/Unionism_in_Scotland "Unionism in Scotland"), support for Scotland remaining part of the UK
* [Unionism in England](/wiki/Unionism_in_England "Unionism in England"), support for England remaining part of the UK
* [Unionism in Wales](/wiki/Unionism_in_Wales "Unionism in Wales"), support for Wales remaining part of the UK

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Unionism&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Education")]

* The organization of students in [students' unions](/wiki/Students%27_union "Students' union")
* [Voluntary student unionism](/wiki/Voluntary_student_unionism "Voluntary student unionism"), a policy under which membership of student unions is voluntary

## Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Unionism&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Religion")]

* Christian [ecumenism](/wiki/Ecumenism "Ecumenism"), initiatives aimed at worldwide Christian unity
